rulings.Case Title: TERESITA GONZALES VS. QUINN GONZALES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Reconsideration Of Order Denying Defendants' Motioan To Set Aside Default Judgment And For Leave To File An Amended Answer, Or In The Alternative Motion For Renewal Of Defendants' Motion To Set Aside Default Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Tuesday, April 15, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANT QUINN GONZALES' Motion for Reconsideration Of Order Denying Defendants' Motion To Set Aside Default Judgment And For Leave To File An Amended Answer, Or In The Alternative Motion For Renewal Of Defendants' Motion To Set Aside Default Judgment. Defendant's Motion for Reconsideration is denied. Defendant does not present new facts, circumstances, or law to warrant reconsideration under Code of Civil Procedure section 1008(a) or (b). rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Cont Depo
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Tuesday, April 15, 2014, Line 8, DEFENDANT BANK OF THE WEST's Motion To Compel Continued Deposition. Granted. Within seven days of the hearing date of April 15, 2014, Plaintiff shall appear for continued deposition unless all parties mutually agree to a later date or Plaintiff's complaint will be stricken. The parties are instructed to meet and confer regarding the deposition venue and date. The deposition shall occur on consecutive days until completed. The deposition shall be limited to the documents requested prior to Plaintiff's first deposition, but produced by Plaintiff after her first deposition. rulings.Case Title: NETSANET HAILEMICHAEL VS. BANK OF THE WEST, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Bank Of The West'S Motion To Compel
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Tuesday, April 15, 2014, Line 8, Defendant Bank Of The West'S Motion To Compel. Granted, in part, and denied in part. Within seven days of the hearing date of April 15, 2014, Plaintiff Hailemichael shall serve further responses by personal delivery, fax or email to Special Interrogatory no. 13. On January 16, 2014, the Court issued an order requiring a further response to Special Interrogatory no. 13. In her Amended Responses, Plaintiff has failed to provide a substantive response. Plaintiff shall provide her own valuation for the emotional distress suffered. (See CACI no. 3905A.) Denied as to Defendant Bank of the West's motion to compel access to Plaintiff's Facebook account. Defendant's request for unlimited access to Plaintiff's Facebook account is beyond the scope of the original document request and is not warranted under the circumstances. Denied as to Defendant 's motion to compel Plaintiff to provide verifications. The verifications are sufficient. Monetary sanctions of $ 2,000.00 are awarded in favor of Defendant Bank of the West and against Plaintiff Netsanet Hailemichael and her counsel to be paid no later than 30 days of service of the notice of entry of order. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: FARMERS INSURANCE EXCHANGE VS. VAN ACKER CONSTRUCTION ASSOCIATES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Against Viking Sandblasting Re The Duty To Defend
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Tuesday, April 15, 2014, Line 9, CROSS COMPLAINANT VAN ACKER CONSTRUCTION ASSOCIATES, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Against Viking Sandblasting Re The Duty To Defend. Granted. (CCP § 437c(f).) Cross-Complainant Van Acker Construction Associates, Inc. ("Van Acker") has met its initial burden to demonstrate that there are no triable issues of fact pertaining to Cross-Defendant Viking Sandblasting ("Viking") duty to defend under the subcontract. "The person indemnifying is bound, on request of the person indemnified, to defend actions or proceedings brought against the latter in respect to the matters embraced by the indemnity, but the person indemnified has the right to conduct such defenses, if he chooses to do so." (CCP § 2778(f).) In this case, the indemnity provision provides the duty to defend. (UMF no. 8; Cross-Complainant's Evidence, Exhibit H.) The allegations of the Complaint are connected to the subject matter of the subcontract, namely the sandblasting activities. (Complaint, 7.) It is undisputed that Van Acker tendered the defense, and Viking has not accepted tender. (UMF nos. 10, 11.) In turn, Viking has not met its shifting burden to demonstrate a triable issue of fact. The collateral source rule does not apply in this instance as Van Acker seeks a ruling on the duty to defend, and not reimbursement of attorney fees. This is factually distinguishable from Bramalea California, Inc. v. Reliable Interiors, Inc. (2004) 119 Cal.App.4th 468, 472. The indemnity provision in the subcontract is clear and explicit, and Viking had a duty to defend from tender. "A contractual promise to "defend" another against specified claims clearly connotes an obligation of active responsibility, from the outset, for the promisee's defense against such claims." (Crawford v. Weather Shield Mfg. Inc. (2008) 44 Cal.4th 541, 553 - 554.) Whether Van Acker was negligent is not at issue in the duty to defend. The duty to defend", as described in the statute, therefore cannot depend on the outcome of that litigation. It follows that, under subdivision 4 of section 2778, claims 'embraced by the indemnity,' as to which the duty to defend is owed, include those which, at the time of tender, allege facts that would give rise to a duty of indemnity." (Id. at 558.) Again, the allegations of the Complaint pertaining to the sandblasting activities triggered the duty to defend based on the language of the indemnity provision. (See Complaint, 7.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: YICHEL CHAN VS. NICHOLAS RAHN VERHAEGHE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Production In Response To Pltf'S Req. For Production Of Documents And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, April 15, 2014, Line 3, PLAINTIFF YICHEL CHAN'S Motion To Compel Production In Response To Plaintiff's Request For Production Of Documents And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Mark Schreiber, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: BENTLY BIOFUELS COMPANY LLC DBA BENTLY BIOFUELS VS. K. DAVID FISHER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Tuesday, April 15, 2014, Line 13, PLAINTIFF BENTLY BIOFUELS COMPANY LLC DBA BENTLY BIOFUELS' Motion For Preliminary Injunction. Granted in part and denied in part. Defendant K. David Fisher and/or any other employee of Defendant 3D Oil & Grease are enjoined from: (1) misrepresenting themselves as having any current affiliation with Bently Biofuels or Got Grease; (2) wearing a Bently Biofuels or Got Grease uniform while performing activities in the scope of their employment at 3D Oil & Grease; (3) moving or altering a Bently Biofuels or Got Grease oil collection container without the express written permission of Bently Biofuels; (4) collecting oil from a Bently Biofuels or Got Grease oil collection container without the express written permission of Bently Biofuels; and (5) collecting oil from an oil collection container that the 3D Oil & Grease employee knows is intended to be collected by Bently Biofuels without the express written permission of Bently Biofuels. Motion denied as to soliciting Bently's customers. Identity of businesses that use cooking oil is not confidential. Bently has produced no evidence that Fisher misappropriated confidential information. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/EHG
